title: puppeteer.launch with ignoreHTTPSErrors: true — the headless browser accepts any TLS certificate (MITM)

params.passDetail: puppeteer.launch validates TLS certificates (ignoreHTTPSErrors is not true) — the headless browser rejects forged or invalid certificates.
params.failDetail: puppeteer.launch is called with ignoreHTTPSErrors: true, so the headless browser accepts ANY TLS certificate — self-signed, expired, or attacker-supplied. When this browser fetches authenticated pages or scrapes data server-side, anyone on the network path can man-in-the-middle it and feed forged content or capture credentials. Remove ignoreHTTPSErrors: true; if a private CA is needed, trust it explicitly.
params.absentCallDetail: No puppeteer launch() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: puppeteer.launch does not set ignoreHTTPSErrors; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: ignoreHTTPSErrors: true is pasted to get past a self-signed cert on a staging box and left in place. In a server-side scraper or SSR renderer that pulls authenticated content, it silently disables MITM protection for every page it loads.
